title: RabbitMQ 4.3 Delayed Retries, Priorities, and Consumer Timeouts · Compiler Case: Delayed Retry Policy
summary: A design copies old TTL dead-letter retry loops into RabbitMQ 4.3, uses priority as unlimited preemption, leaves consumer timeouts unrelated to handler deadlines, and assumes new broker behavior exists on older clusters. Planned artifact: Add and verify the delayed retry policy increment in RabbitMQ 4.3 feature and fallback compatibility plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum delayed-retry policies for returned or failed deliveries with explicit delay, eligibility, attempt, and compatibility evidence.
Plan majority loss, delayed confirms, redelivery, operator checks, replica repair, and residual data-risk evidence without unsafe force recovery.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Delayed Retry Policy · Theory Delayed Retry Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 7832 in a quorum queue during a planned rolling maintenance window. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum retry, priority, and timeout behavior on version and valid budgets; keep reconnection and retry work cancellable under one deadline; then remove the dead-letter binding and expose the resulting retention risk. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum delayed-retry policies for returned or failed deliveries with explicit delay, eligibility, attempt, and compatibility evidence.
